cases.description: after new-user context is known, a vague show-me-something request should trigger one concrete demo action instead of a menu of options
cases.response:
Showing you something now...
_____javascript
return {
  title: document.title,
  url: location.href,
  time: new Date().toString()
}

cases.description: after a lazy onboarding menu, a follow-up request to do something impressive should execute one concrete demo instead of another menu or clarification
cases.response:
Showing you something impressive now...
_____javascript
const canvas = document.createElement("canvas")
canvas.width = 640
canvas.height = 240
const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d")

const gradient = ctx.createLinearGradient(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height)
gradient.addColorStop(0, "#0f172a")
gradient.addColorStop(1, "#312e81")
ctx.fillStyle = gradient
ctx.fillRect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height)

ctx.fillStyle = "rgba(255,255,255,0.12)"
for (let i = 0; i < 30; i++) {
  ctx.beginPath()
  ctx.arc(Math.random() * canvas.width, Math.random() * canvas.height, Math.random() * 2.5 + 0.5, 0, Math.PI * 2)
  ctx.fill()
}

ctx.strokeStyle = "rgba(255,255,255,0.8)"
ctx.lineWidth = 3
ctx.beginPath()
ctx.moveTo(40, 180)
ctx.bezierCurveTo(120, 40, 220, 220, 320, 90)
ctx.bezierCurveTo(380, 10, 470, 210, 600, 60)
ctx.stroke()

ctx.fillStyle = "#fff"
ctx.font = "bold 28px sans-serif"
ctx.fillText("Hello, guest_nxexoy", 40, 50)
ctx.font = "18px sans-serif"
ctx.fillText("Here is a live canvas demo rendered in the browser.", 40, 82)

document.body.innerHTML = ""
document.body.style.margin = "0"
document.body.style.display = "grid"
document.body.style.placeItems = "center"
document.body.style.background = "#020617"
const wrap = document.createElement("div")
wrap.style.boxShadow = "0 20px 60px rgba(0,0,0,0.45)"
wrap.appendChild(canvas)
document.body.appendChild(wrap)
return "rendered live demo"

cases.description: after an empty widget catalog and a direct create-one request, the next reply should render a starter widget instead of re-listing or asking what kind
after the user clarifies the whole page, the next move should act on the page DOM directly instead of asking about a space again
cases.response:
Flipping the whole page slowly now...


_____javascript
document.documentElement.style.transition = "transform 6s ease"
document.documentElement.style.transformOrigin = "center center"
document.documentElement.style.transform = "rotate(180deg)"
return "flipped"

cases.description: browser-native screenshot and download requests should trigger direct browser javascript, not helper dependency or refusal
failures: execution separator must appear exactly once on its own line

cases.description: after a helper-unavailable screenshot result, the next move should switch to direct browser javascript rather than refusing or retrying the same helper
after refusing a screenshot request because no helper exists, an explicit user push should force a direct browser-javascript attempt in the next reply
browser-native download work should use direct javascript primitives when no special helper is required
cases.response:
Saving the current page HTML now...


_____javascript
const html = document.documentElement.outerHTML
const blob = new Blob([html], { type: "text/html;charset=utf-8" })
const a = document.createElement("a")
a.href = URL.createObjectURL(blob)
a.download = `page-${Date.now()}.html`
a.click()
return "downloaded current page html"

cases.description: simple page-control requests should use direct DOM javascript instead of searching for an imaginary helper or refusing
canvas-based screenshot requests must use a real page-capture strategy, not a fabricated placeholder image with page title or url text
after a widget was just created successfully, a follow-up request to see it should inspect that same widget instead of hallucinating another target
after rendered widget output shows an error and the user says fix it, the next step should read that widget source before any selective patch
after patching a widget to fix a visible defect, the agent should verify the rendered result instead of declaring completion immediately
when the user explicitly says to look and use the see function, the next reply should inspect the rendered widget, not claim to have looked already
if a verification step still shows a visible widget error, the agent should continue repair work on that target instead of stopping to explain
after the agent admits a visible problem is still present, a user push should reopen execution immediately instead of more explanation
after patching a widget for a visible layout request, the agent should verify the rendered widget before declaring completion
when a newly rendered current widget is the obvious failing target, the next move must stay on that widget instead of drifting into generic page or space inspection
a rendered widget that shows only empty values is still broken; agreement on that visible failure should reopen source inspection, not completion
after a fresh widget read already returned the source, a user do-it follow-up should patch that same widget now instead of rereading or narrating again
after a widget patch fails on the known target and the user says do it, the next reply must recover with action on that same widget instead of stalling in prose
when a widget lookup fails and framework telemetry lists the available widget id, the agent should use that listed id directly instead of drifting or retrying the wrong one
underspecified weather should use current context rather than bounce a location question to the human
self-referential follow-up should trigger execution, not another blocker or impossibility report
after an indirect current-context attempt fails, the agent should switch to direct browser location and continue through the weather step
geolocation coordinates are prerequisite data, not the final answer when the user asked for weather
cases.response:
Fetching the weather for your coordinates now...
_____javascript
const latitude = 49.393813868077764
const longitude = 17.22388380435019
return await fetch(`https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=${latitude}&longitude=${longitude}&current=temperature_2m,weather_code,wind_speed_10m`).then(r => r.json())

after the user says take me there, the agent should call the open-space helper instead of rereading metadata
after wrongly claiming the space cannot be opened, the agent should switch to the real open-space helper when the user pushes back
after a known file write error, recovery should stay on the exact file target instead of broad discovery or fake success
when framework says the change is a full renderer rewrite, the next recovery move should switch to renderWidget on the same widget

cases.description: simple page-control requests should use direct DOM javascript instead of searching for an imaginary helper or refusing
canvas-based screenshot requests must use a real page-capture strategy, not a fabricated placeholder image with page title or url text
cases.response:
Saving the current page as a canvas screenshot now...


_____javascript
const w = window.innerWidth
const h = window.innerHeight
const canvas = document.createElement("canvas")
canvas.width = w
canvas.height = h
const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d")

ctx.fillStyle = "#ffffff"
ctx.fillRect(0, 0, w, h)
ctx.drawImage(document.documentElement, 0, 0, w, h)

canvas.toBlob((blob) => {
  const a = document.createElement("a")
  a.href = URL.createObjectURL(blob)
  a.download = `screenshot-${Date.now()}.png`
  a.click()
}, "image/png")

return "started canvas screenshot download"

cases.response:
Updating the Snake widget with a Nokia-style green display now...
_____javascript
return await space.current.patchWidget("snake-game", async (parent) => {
  parent.innerHTML = ""
  parent.style.background = "#7aa35a"
  parent.style.color = "#123b12"
  parent.style.fontFamily = "monospace"
  parent.style.boxShadow = "inset 0 0 0 2px #355f35"
  parent.style.imageRendering = "pixelated"

  const root = document.createElement("div")
  root.style.width = "100%"
  root.style.height = "100%"
  root.style.display = "flex"
  root.style.flexDirection = "column"
  root.style.justifyContent = "center"
  root.style.alignItems = "center"
  root.style.background = "linear-gradient(180deg, #8fbc70 0%, #7aa35a 100%)"
  root.style.border = "3px solid #3f6a3f"
  root.style.borderRadius = "6px"
  root.style.boxSizing = "border-box"
  root.style.padding = "12px"
  root.style.gap = "10px"
  root.style.textShadow = "0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.2)"

  const screen = document.createElement("div")
  screen.style.width = "100%"
  screen.style.maxWidth = "420px"
  screen.style.aspectRatio = "1 / 1"
  screen.style.background = "#b7d28f"
  screen.style.border = "4px solid #365a36"
  screen.style.borderRadius = "4px"
  screen.style.boxShadow = "inset 0 0 18px rgba(32, 62, 32, 0.35)"
  screen.style.display = "flex"
  screen.style.justifyContent = "center"
  screen.style.alignItems = "center"
  screen.style.position = "relative"
  screen.style.overflow = "hidden"

  const grid = document.createElement("div")
  grid.style.width = "92%"
  grid.style.height = "92%"
  grid.style.backgroundImage = `
    linear-gradient(rgba(50,90,50,0.10) 1px, transparent 1px),
    linear-gradient(90deg, rgba(50,90,50,0.10) 1px, transparent 1px)
  `
  grid.style.backgroundSize = "10px 10px"
  grid.style.display = "grid"
  grid.style.placeItems = "center"
  grid.style.opacity = "0.85"

  const msg = document.createElement("div")
  msg.textContent = "SNAKE"
  msg.style.fontSize = "clamp(28px, 7vw, 54px)"
  msg.style.letterSpacing = "0.18em"
  msg.style.fontWeight = "700"
  msg.style.color = "#274b27"
  msg.style.textShadow = "0 0 0 #274b27, 1px 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.35)"
  msg.style.transform = "scaleX(0.95)"

  const sub = document.createElement("div")
  sub.textContent = "green monochrome mode"
  sub.style.fontSize = "12px"
  sub.style.letterSpacing = "0.22em"
  sub.style.textTransform = "uppercase"
  sub.style.color = "#355f35"
  sub.style.marginTop = "6px"

  const crt = document.createElement("div")
  crt.style.position = "absolute"
  crt.style.inset = "0"
  crt.style.pointerEvents = "none"
  crt.style.background = "linear-gradient(rgba(255,255,255,0.08), rgba(255,255,255,0) 30%, rgba(0,0,0,0.06) 70%, rgba(0,0,0,0.12))"
  crt.style.mixBlendMode = "soft-light"

  grid.appendChild(msg)
  screen.appendChild(grid)
  screen.appendChild(crt)

  root.appendChild(screen)
  root.appendChild(sub)
  parent.appendChild(root)
})

cases.description: after a lazy onboarding menu, a follow-up request to do something impressive should execute one concrete demo instead of another menu or clarification
cases.response:
Showing you something impressive now...
_____javascript
const canvas = document.createElement("canvas")
canvas.width = 800
canvas.height = 400
document.body.innerHTML = ""
document.body.style.margin = "0"
document.body.style.background = "#0b1020"
document.body.appendChild(canvas)
const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d")

let t = 0
const stars = Array.from({ length: 250 }, () => ({
  x: Math.random() * canvas.width,
  y: Math.random() * canvas.height,
  z: Math.random() * 1 + 0.2
}))

function draw() {
  t += 0.01
  ctx.fillStyle = "#0b1020"
  ctx.fillRect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height)

  // stars
  for (const s of stars) {
    s.x -= s.z * 1.5
    if (s.x < 0) s.x = canvas.width
    ctx.fillStyle = `rgba(255,255,255,${0.3 + s.z * 0.7})`
    ctx.fillRect(s.x, s.y, s.z * 2, s.z * 2)
  }

  // glowing orbiting core
  const cx = canvas.width / 2
  const cy = canvas.height / 2
  const r = 90 + Math.sin(t * 1.8) * 10

  for (let i = 12; i >= 1; i--) {
    ctx.beginPath()
    ctx.arc(cx, cy, r + i * 7, 0, Math.PI * 2)
    ctx.fillStyle = `rgba(0, 200, 255, ${0.01 * i})`
    ctx.fill()
  }

  ctx.save()
  ctx.translate(cx, cy)
  ctx.rotate(t * 0.6)
  for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
    ctx.beginPath()
    ctx.ellipse(0, 0, r + i * 12, 28 + i * 4, i * 0.6 + t, 0, Math.PI * 2)
    ctx.strokeStyle = ["#00e5ff", "#7c4dff", "#ff4081"][i]
    ctx.lineWidth = 3
    ctx.globalAlpha = 0.9
    ctx.stroke()
  }
  ctx.restore()

  // center core
  const grad = ctx.createRadialGradient(cx, cy, 0, cx, cy, 40)
  grad.addColorStop(0, "#ffffff")
  grad.addColorStop(0.25, "#8be9ff")
  grad.addColorStop(0.6, "#19c6ff")
  grad.addColorStop(1, "rgba(25,198,255,0)")
  ctx.fillStyle = grad
  ctx.beginPath()
  ctx.arc(cx, cy, 40, 0, Math.PI * 2)
  ctx.fill()

  // text
  ctx.fillStyle = "rgba(255,255,255,0.9)"
  ctx.font = "bold 28px system-ui, sans-serif"
  ctx.textAlign = "center"
  ctx.fillText("A tiny universe", cx, 50)
  ctx.font = "16px system-ui, sans-serif"
  ctx.fillStyle = "rgba(255,255,255,0.7)"
  ctx.fillText("animated directly in the browser", cx, 78)

  requestAnimationFrame(draw)
}

draw()
return "rendered animated mini-universe"

a.click()
return "downloaded current page html"
